title: Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race
summary: Blue Origin has entered the orbital data center market by proposing a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. This move positions the company to compete in the emerging space-based data infrastructure sector.

title: Register now: The energy imperative driving the push toward orbital data centers
summary: A virtual event on March 31, sponsored by Star Catcher and the Commercial Space Federation, will discuss the energy-driven push for orbital data centers. The event highlights the growing interest in space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Kratos wins $446 million Space Force contract for missile-tracking ground systems
summary: Kratos secured a $446 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop ground management and integration systems for the Resilient Missile Warning and Tracking satellite program. This deal highlights Kratos' role in enhancing missile-tracking capabilities for national security.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service
summary: Portal Space Systems is collaborating with Australian startup Paladin Space to launch a commercial orbital debris removal service. This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris through innovative spacecraft technology.

title: Eileen Collins on what it takes to become Space Shuttle Commander
summary: Eileen Collins, the first woman to pilot the Space Shuttle, discusses the requirements and experiences needed to become a Space Shuttle Commander in a Space Minds podcast interview. The episode highlights her career as a retired NASA astronaut and Air Force colonel.

title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has generated both excitement and questions within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. This deal marks a significant transaction in the satellite manufacturing and asset-financing sectors.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract to supply a satellite bus for a Japanese technology demonstration mission. This deal marks Apex's expansion into the Japanese space market.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract from the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 50 commercial players
summary: Space Command is hosting a classified wargame that will include 50 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance integration between military and commercial space sectors.

title: These cotton candy exoplanets hide behind a haze even the James Webb Space Telescope can't penetrate
summary: Scientists have discovered exoplanets so low in density they resemble cotton candy, with atmospheres obscured by an impenetrable haze even the James Webb Space Telescope cannot see through.

title: 'A great relief!' Europe's Proba-3 solar-eclipse satellite phones home after a month of silence
summary: Europe's Proba-3 solar-eclipse satellite reestablished contact after a month of silence. The mission consists of two spacecraft designed to study solar eclipses.

title: Earthshine will reveal the moon's hidden face this week — here's how to see it
summary: Earthshine will make the moon's hidden face visible this week, offering a unique viewing opportunity. The phenomenon occurs when sunlight reflects off Earth, illuminating the moon's dark side.

title: Private South Korean rocket exploded last December due to hardware failure, investigation finds
summary: South Korean startup Innospace's first orbital launch attempt failed due to a hardware failure, resulting in the explosion of its private rocket last December. The launch, which carried five satellites, took place from Brazil on December 22.

title: Lego's first-ever Tintin set is coming next month and it's a space-themed Lego Tintin Moon rocket
summary: Lego is releasing a new space-themed Tintin Moon Rocket set featuring six minifigures on April 1. The set combines popular franchises Lego and Tintin with a space exploration theme.

title: I talked to Andy Weir about the astrobiology behind 'Project Hail Mary'
summary: Andy Weir discusses the astrobiology behind 'Project Hail Mary,' exploring the scientific plausibility of alien life in the new film. The analysis compares the movie's concepts with real-world astrobiological research.

title: NASA peers inside an asteroid | Space photo of the day for March 19, 2026
summary: NASA has used advanced imaging techniques to peer inside samples of asteroid Bennu, discovering extensive networks of cracks running throughout the rock particles. This discovery could provide insights into the asteroid's formation and history.

title: Ohio fireball may have dropped meteorites between Akron and Canton — should you pick it up if you find one?
summary: A meteor exploded over Ohio with the force of 250 tons of TNT, potentially dropping meteorites between Akron and Canton. Experts advise leaving any found meteorites untouched to preserve scientific value.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: An AI-driven cyberattack could potentially hijack satellites, causing collisions and rendering Earth's orbit unsafe within the next two years. The industry is questioning whether current defenses are adequate to prevent such a 'satellite apocalypse.'

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit on March 20
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' on March 20. The launch can be watched live.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano in Hawaii entered its 43rd eruption phase, producing high lava fountains and spreading ash widely. This marks a significant escalation in the ongoing volcanic activity.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida documented an American bald eagle flying from its nest during annual nesting surveys. The surveys are conducted to track active and inactive bald eagle nests across Kennedy Space Center, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore.

title: How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets
summary: NASA is collecting explosion data to enhance the safety of next-generation rockets, particularly those using liquid oxygen and methane propulsion. Engineers with extensive cryogenic and test operations expertise are leading this effort as propulsion systems scale up.

title: NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate
summary: NASA's laser reflecting technology, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, is now operational as of March 9, enhancing GPS accuracy. The instrument, called a laser retroreflector array (LRA), was part of the U.S. Space Force's Block III GPS satellite deployed on January 27.

title: NASA to Cover Progress 94 Spacecraft Launch, Space Station Docking
summary: NASA will broadcast the launch and docking of Roscosmos' Progress 94 cargo spacecraft, which is delivering supplies to the International Space Station. The unpiloted spacecraft is set to launch on March 22 and carry three tons of food, fuel, and other essentials.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, selected in 2025, has been named 'the Platypi' as they train for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars. The ten explorers are developing technical and operational skills at NASA's Johnson Space Center.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure and vibration simulations, confirming its readiness for launch conditions. The mission remains on track for an early launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Abundant rainfall in February and March 2026 turned Central Australia's desert landscape green. This unusual weather event significantly altered the region's ecosystem.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by a team including Catherine O'Connell-Cooper from the University of New Brunswick, Canada.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: NASA's Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon volcano in the Philippines on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. PHIVOLCS reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during this event.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches highlight a busy period for the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept combines various technologies to address potential asteroid threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its goals of sending humans to the Moon and Mars by transforming steel rolls into Starship at its Starfactory facility. The article highlights the company's rapid progress in spacecraft development.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: A new crew arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February following the early departure of Crew-11 in January. The month was marked by new crew arrivals and scientific activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket set to dominate the launch schedule. The post highlights the busy week ahead in space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since its last flight over 147 days ago. This development signals progress in SpaceX's reusable rocket technology.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Firefly Alpha FLTA007 'Stairway to Seven' rocket, marking a return to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. This launch demonstrates Firefly's recovery and progress in the commercial space industry.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This repair marks a critical step in maintaining Russia's ISS resupply capabilities.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced significant changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum, though key details are still being developed.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on the space industry without specifying a particular news item or event.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful reconnection.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to engage industry stakeholders and provide updates on ESA's planned activities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for a January 2027 launch aboard an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a football-sized, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. This innovation addresses the growing risks of disruption to critical satellite navigation systems.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research and hands-on training for future space missions.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on an operational debris removal service, emphasizing the importance of active debris management for critical satellite infrastructure. Portal CEO Jeff Thornburg highlighted the shift from experimental to practical solutions in space debris mitigation.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital SR75 flight, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year. This deal marks Apex's entry into the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket, modified after an anomaly, is expected to launch this summer with improvements to its nozzle and solid rocket booster to enhance margins. United Launch Alliance (ULA) is accelerating these changes to expedite the rocket's readiness.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights the growing interest in alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another milestone in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for US launchers to comply with updated licensing requirements introduced five years ago. All operators are now adhering to the new Part 450 regulations.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to keep up with growing demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to two major industry groups.

title: Nvidia Unveils New Hardware to Unlock Orbital Data Centers
summary: Nvidia has introduced new hardware designed to enable the development of orbital data centers, which could revolutionize data processing in space. The announcement provides insights into the future architecture of space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Canadian Launchers and Spaceport Get Funding Boost
summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to enhance its sovereign launch capabilities. This funding boost aims to strengthen the country's space industry.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for 50% revenue growth in 2026 after restructuring in 2025, including divesting its maritime business and reducing debt. CEO Theresa Condor outlined this target to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic focus on OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand amid a pivotal phase in its history.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris through repeatable missions.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI is set to purchase two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by the companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. This delivery marks a significant step in modernizing the Army's communication infrastructure.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America, emphasizing securing critical infrastructure and advancing emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum computing.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract from the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at an event in London, marking four years since Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted evolving military strategies and challenges.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has assured the FCC that its SkyTerra Next proposal, involving an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, will not cause interference. The filing emphasizes compliance with existing L-band coordination.
